title: ferray-fft: nd.rs lane extraction copies each lane individually — O(n) allocations for ND FFT
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

fft_along_axis in nd.rs extracts each 1D lane via 'let mut buffer = Vec::with_capacity(fft_len); for i in 0..axis_len { buffer.push(data[start_offset + i * stride]); }'. For an (M, N) array with FFT along axis 1, this does M separate Vec allocations. NumPy's pocketfft uses stride-aware in-place transforms that don't extract lanes at all. While the per-lane extraction enables Rayon parallelism (which is good), the allocation overhead is significant for many short lanes. Consider pre-allocating a lane buffer per thread (map_init already does this for scratch, extend it to the lane buffer).
